title: along with the share code, I also want autosync feature using firebase to syn...
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

along with the share code, I also want autosync feature using firebase to sync across devices. If there are credentials needed, Claude can suggest it in the output for the manual steps.

---

**Category:** `enhancement`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
Feature Request: Firebase Autosync for Cross-Device Data Sync

What the user reported:
- The app currently has a "share code" feature for cross-device data sharing
- The user wants an additional automatic sync feature using Firebase Realtime Database or Firestore
- If Firebase credentials are needed, the implementation should output manual setup steps

Component/area affected:
- src/ (main app logic, data persistence layer)
- Currently the app uses IndexedDB (local storage) for all baby tracking data
- New: Firebase sync layer on top of existing storage

Expected behaviour:
- When autosync is enabled, any new log entry (feed, sleep, diaper, etc.) is automatically synced to Firebase
- Multiple devices sharing the same Firebase project/sync key can see each other's data in real time
- Data should merge gracefully (no overwrite conflicts between devices)
- Works offline and syncs when connectivity is restored (Firebase handles this natively)

Current behaviour:
- Data is stored locally in IndexedDB only
- Cross-device sharing requires manual export/import via the share code feature

Edge cases to handle:
- Offline-first: data should still be writable offline and sync when online
- Conflict resolution: if two devices log simultaneously, use timestamp-based merge (last write wins per entry key)
- Auth: Firebase anonymous auth or a user-provided project config (no email/password required)
- Credentials: provide manual setup instructions as code comments or console output (e.g. firebaseConfig object that the user pastes in)
- Graceful degradation: if Firebase is not configured, app works exactly as before

Acceptance criteria:
1. A Firebase sync module is added to src/ that integrates with the existing data layer
2. When Firebase is configured (user provides config), all log entries are synced in real time
3. Sync is opt-in — existing functionality is unchanged if Firebase is not configured
4. Manual setup instructions are included (either in code comments or a displayed setup guide)
5. The share code feature continues to work independently

Testing:
- Unit test: sync module correctly reads/writes to Firebase mock
- Unit test: offline writes queue and flush correctly when online
- Unit test: conflict resolution (last-write-wins by timestamp) works correctly
- Unit test: sync disabled gracefully when no Firebase config present
- Integration: two simulated devices sharing same sync key see same data
failure_reason: Claude CLI exited with code 143. Check /Users/akashkg/saanvi/babybloom/bot/claude-issue.log for details.

body:

c6a88e2 Fix quick log warning items hidden from top 8 view (closes #151)

3. Pipeline will auto-close this issue on next successful deploy

---
_Auto-created by BabyBloom pipeline on deploy failure._
enhanced_description:
Deploy Failure: GitHub Actions "Install dependencies" step failed on commit f865200

What was reported:
- The GitHub Actions CI pipeline failed on the "test" job at the "Install dependencies" step
- Commit: f865200 (Auto-deploy: update BabyBloom)
- Time: 2026-03-29 15:06 PDT
- Actions run: https://github.com/akashkguw/babybloom/actions/runs/23720191130

Component/area affected:
- .github/workflows/ — CI/CD pipeline configuration
- package.json / package-lock.json — dependency definitions
- Recent commits include implementation of bath/sleep mutual exclusions (#153) which may have introduced a dependency change

Expected behaviour:
- npm install (or equivalent) completes successfully in the GitHub Actions runner
- All test and build steps pass, enabling deployment

Current behaviour:
- The "Install dependencies" step fails in GitHub Actions job "test (20)"
- Deploy is blocked

Likely causes to investigate:
1. package-lock.json out of sync or corrupted after recent changes
2. A newly added dependency not available on the Actions runner platform/Node version
3. Network/registry timeout in GitHub Actions (transient)
4. package.json references a package version that does not exist or was unpublished

Edge cases to handle:
- If it is a transient network failure, re-running the workflow may resolve it
- If it is a package version mismatch, package-lock.json needs to be regenerated
- Ensure Node.js version in the workflow matches local dev environment

Acceptance criteria:
1. "Install dependencies" step passes in GitHub Actions
2. All subsequent CI steps (unit tests, build, smoke test) also pass
3. Deployment proceeds successfully on next commit

Testing:
- Verify npm install succeeds locally with the same Node version as the Actions runner
- Check that package-lock.json is committed and consistent with package.json
- Re-run the Actions workflow to confirm the fix
implementation_notes:
WHAT YOU ACTUALLY DID:
- Files changed: package-lock.json — regenerated to include firebase@12.11.0 and all @firebase/* sub-packages
- Approach taken: The committed package-lock.json was missing firebase entries while package.json already declared firebase@^12.11.0. npm ci in GitHub Actions requires package.json and package-lock.json to be in sync. The local package-lock.json already had the correct firebase entries from a previous npm install; it just had not been committed. Staged and committed the updated lock file.
- Testing done: npm ci --dry-run (success), npm run test (455/455 passed), node tests/regression.cjs (21/21 passed), npm run type-check (clean), npm run build (success)
- Any manual steps needed: None — next push will trigger CI which should now pass
